mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day return frequency drops below 55% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate equity sell-offs as investors price in imminent recessionary earnings declines.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or the return differential fails to reach statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which compounds corporate revenue expectations and drives sustained positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to produce a statistically significant positive return differential (p > 0.05) over 60-day horizons in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints and weakening output, forcing equity valuations lower as growth expectations are downgraded.
failure_condition: Industrial production contractions during tightening cycles yield positive 20-day forward returns in > 58% of cases, or the mechanism shows negative predictive power (Rank IC < -0.01).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in the consumer price index below 3.0% signals disinflationary momentum that removes tail risk from equity valuations, triggering immediate positive market repricing over the following five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of triggered observations, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) across rolling 2-year out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment rates indicates structural labor market weakness rather than cyclical friction, which systematically depresses corporate profit margins and yields negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern fails to predict negative returns with a Rank IC below -0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive return frequency exceeds 50% in triggered samples.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product directly expands aggregate corporate revenue pools, providing a fundamental earnings floor that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth correlates with negative 20-day forward returns in > 55% of observations, or the predictive Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all tested walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: An expansion in the index of industrial production during a post-shock recovery regime confirms supply chain normalization and capacity utilization gains, which immediately boosts equity valuations over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production gains in recovery regimes produce negative 5-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or the mechanism yields a negative Rank IC in out-of-sample validation.
